channel.title: AI Builder's Field Guide — Recently Added
channel.description: A daily-curated wiki of the tools, models, and patterns that AI power-users and builders actually reach for.

item.title: Rayfin
item.description: An open-source SDK and CLI that enables developers and coding agents to define and deploy complete application backends in code. — It simplifies backend development by automating database, authentication, and API provisioning, allowing rapid movement from prototype to production with enterprise-grade security on Microsoft Fabric.

item.title: SubQ (by Subquadratic)
item.description: A large language model built on a fully sub-quadratic sparse-attention architecture, offering a native 12 million token context window. — It provides significantly increased context windows and faster inference at a much lower cost than traditional transformer-based models, making long-context reasoning practical for agents.

item.title: Markitdown (Microsoft)
item.description: A lightweight Python tool that converts various document formats like PDFs, Word, and Excel into clean Markdown for AI processing. — This tool simplifies preparing unstructured data for AI, enabling coding agents to more effectively analyze and summarize content compared to feeding raw, complex document formats. It addresses a common data preparation bottleneck for AI builders.

item.title: CoreMem
item.description: CoreMem allows users to build and share portable collections of context, or 'mems,' with any AI agent via URL, extensions, or the Model Context Protocol. — It provides a persistent and shareable memory layer, solving the problem of AI agents losing context across different sessions or when switching between various tools. This is crucial for building reliable and consistent agentic workflows.

item.title: DeepEval
item.description: DeepEval is an open-source, Python-first LLM evaluation framework that provides a pytest-native testing experience for AI applications. — It offers over 50 research-backed metrics for evaluating RAG, agents, and chatbots, enabling developers to integrate LLM evaluation into CI/CD workflows.

item.title: Luma Dream Machine
item.description: Luma Dream Machine generates realistic, high-quality videos from text or image prompts, with capabilities like cinematic camera movements. — It offers native 1080p output and a Draft Mode for previewing low-resolution shots, which helps creators iterate efficiently and save resources.

item.title: Velo
item.description: Velo is an AI video creation platform that transforms raw screen recordings into polished, shareable video messages with automated scripts and AI avatars. — It simplifies video production for demos and explainers by automating scriptwriting and avatar generation, reducing the need for extensive editing compared to traditional video tools.

item.title: Kilo Code
item.description: Kilo Code is an open-source AI coding agent for IDEs and CLI that generates code, automates tasks, and debugs across over 500 models. — It offers an open and flexible alternative to proprietary AI coding assistants by supporting a wide range of models and providing deep integration into developer workflows.
